basic_details.job_title: BHEL Haridwar Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026: 187 Vacancies, Apply Online Before 15 July
basic_details.short_information: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL), a Maharatna Central Public Sector Undertaking, has released a notification for engagement of Trade Apprentices at its Haridwar unit for the year 2026-27. The recruitment is conducted under the guidance of the Human Resources Department, BHEL Ranipur, Haridwar, Uttarakhand.

important_dates.raw: Start date for online application: 02 July 2026 Last date for online application: 15 July 2026 Last date to submit acknowledgement slip with documents: 27 July 2026 Expected joining of selected candidates: September 2026

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on 01 July 2026.

qualification.details: Candidate must have passed High School (Class 10). Candidate must have passed ITI (Industrial Training Institute) in the relevant trade from an institute recognized by NCVT (National Council for Vocational Training). For Welder and Electrician trades, ITI passed from an institute recognized by NCVT or SCVT (State Council for Vocational Training) is also accepted. Minimum marks required in ITI: General / EWS / OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) category: minimum 60% SC / ST category: minimum 55%

salary_details.details: All selected candidates will be paid a monthly stipend during the training period as per applicable apprenticeship guidelines. The exact stipend amount will be as prescribed under the Apprentices Act and related rules.

selection_process: Selection will be based on a merit list prepared using a weighted average formula:
70% weightage to marks obtained in ITI 30% weightage to marks obtained in High School
In case of a tie in weighted average marks
preference will be given to the candidate with a higher ITI percentage. If the tie persists
preference will be given to the older candidate (higher age). If the number of eligible candidates in a trade is less than or equal to the number of vacancies
all eligible candidates will be called directly for training.
how_to_apply: The application process has two steps:

Candidates must first register on the National Apprenticeship Training Portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. All details, including the Aadhaar number, must be entered carefully. The name, father's name, and date of birth in Aadhaar must match exactly, and the portal must show successful Aadhaar verification.

After registering on the Apprenticeship Portal, candidates must complete the online application form under the link "Engagement of Trade Apprenticeship for 2026, BHEL Haridwar" on the BHEL Haridwar career website: hwr.bhel.com/recruitment.
Fill in all personal details accurately, including name, father's name, date of birth, category, High School and ITI marks.
Upload a recent passport-size photograph (.jpg/.jpeg, up to 30 KB).
Upload the required documents in PDF format (up to 500 KB).
The last date to submit the online application is 15 July 2026.

After successfully submitting the online form, candidates must take a printout of the Acknowledgement Slip and send it, along with self-attested photocopies of the required documents, in an envelope marked "Trade Apprentice Training 2026 Application Form and Trade Name" to the address below by post or in person, on or before 27 July 2026:
Karyapalak (Ma.Sam.) Room No. 29, Human Resources – Recruitment Section Main Administrative Building, BHEL, HEEP Ranipur, Haridwar (Uttarakhand) – 249403
important_instructions: Applications received without complete details, without the required uploaded documents, or without submission of the acknowledgement slip and documents will be rejected. Candidates who have already completed apprenticeship training and were terminated from another organization within three months prior to joining BHEL are not eligible under the Apprentices (Amendment) Rules, 2025. Dependents (spouse/children) of BHEL Haridwar employees get a 3-year relaxation in the ITI passing year requirement, subject to RDSDE online approval; registration on the Apprenticeship Portal is still mandatory. No travelling allowance or dearness allowance will be paid to candidates called for joining/reporting. Selected candidates must make their own arrangements for stay and food during the training period. This training does not confer any right or claim to regular employment at BHEL. Any corrigendum, correction, or update related to this recruitment will be published only on the official BHEL Haridwar career website; no separate notice will be given through newspapers or other media. Management reserves the right to modify, postpone, cancel, or suspend the selection process at any time without assigning any reason, and its decision will be final. Any dispute arising out of this notification will fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of courts/tribunals in Haridwar.

content_sections.heading: BHEL Haridwar Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Overview
content_sections.text: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL), a Maharatna Central Public Sector Undertaking, has released a notification for engagement of Trade Apprentices at its Haridwar unit for the year 2026-27. The recruitment is conducted under the guidance of the Human Resources Department, BHEL Ranipur, Haridwar, Uttarakhand.

content_sections.heading: BHEL Haridwar Apprentice 2026: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Start date for online application: 02 July 2026 Last date for online application: 15 July 2026 Last date to submit acknowledgement slip with documents: 27 July 2026 Expected joining of selected candidates: September 2026

content_sections.heading: BHEL Haridwar Trade Apprentice Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: BHEL Haridwar has announced 187 apprentice vacancies across seven trades. The number of vacancies is provisional and may change based on final assessment.

content_sections.heading: BHEL Haridwar Apprentice Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates who have passed ITI in the relevant trade from an NCVT-recognized institute are eligible to apply. Candidates who have already completed apprenticeship training after ITI, or who have one year or more of job experience, or who are pursuing any regular course, are not eligible to apply. Candidates must be regular ITI pass-outs from an NCVT or SCVT recognized institute for the years 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026, depending on the trade. Candidates who have undergone apprenticeship as a private candidate are not eligible. Only candidates who passed ITI in 2023 or later, with a marksheet issued by the board, are eligible to apply.

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification for BHEL Trade Apprentice 2026
content_sections.text: Candidate must have passed High School (Class 10). Candidate must have passed ITI (Industrial Training Institute) in the relevant trade from an institute recognized by NCVT (National Council for Vocational Training). For Welder and Electrician trades, ITI passed from an institute recognized by NCVT or SCVT (State Council for Vocational Training) is also accepted. Minimum marks required in ITI: General / EWS / OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) category: minimum 60% SC / ST category: minimum 55%

content_sections.heading: BHEL Haridwar Apprentice Age Limit 2026
content_sections.text: Age is calculated as on 01 July 2026.

rows.maximum_age: Additional 10 years relaxation in respective category

content_sections.heading: BHEL Haridwar Trade Apprentice Stipend
content_sections.text: All selected candidates will be paid a monthly stipend during the training period as per applicable apprenticeship guidelines. The exact stipend amount will be as prescribed under the Apprentices Act and related rules.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy
content_sections.text: Reservation will be provided as per government norms for SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), EWS, and PwD candidates. OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates must submit a caste certificate issued on or after 01 April 2025; older or expired certificates will not be accepted, and the certificate must be in the format prescribed by the Government of India. EWS candidates must submit an EWS certificate valid for financial year 2026-27. Reservation will be provided to dependents of BHEL Haridwar employees (working/retired/deceased) as per company rules. Priority will be given to candidates domiciled in Uttarakhand, for which a valid Uttarakhand Domicile Certificate is mandatory (not required for dependents of BHEL Haridwar employees).

content_sections.heading: BHEL Haridwar Apprentice Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: Selection will be based on a merit list prepared using a weighted average formula: 70% weightage to marks obtained in ITI 30% weightage to marks obtained in High School In case of a tie in weighted average marks, preference will be given to the candidate with a higher ITI percentage. If the tie persists, preference will be given to the older candidate (higher age). If the number of eligible candidates in a trade is less than or equal to the number of vacancies, all eligible candidates will be called directly for training.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for BHEL Haridwar Apprentice Application
content_sections.text: The following documents must be uploaded online (Part 2 of the application) and later submitted physically along with the acknowledgement slip: Latest passport-size colour photograph (.jpg/.jpeg, up to 30 KB) High School (Class 10) certificate ITI consolidated marksheet (or all semester marksheets if consolidated marksheet is unavailable) Aadhaar Card Caste certificate / EWS certificate (if applicable) Disability certificate (if applicable) Uttarakhand Domicile Certificate (mandatory for Uttarakhand residents; not required for dependents of BHEL Haridwar employees) Registration proof from the Apprenticeship Portal (profile printout with name, registration number, date of birth, etc.) "Form for Employee Ward" (only applicable for dependents of BHEL Haridwar employees) All documents (except the photograph) must be uploaded in PDF format, up to 500 KB each.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for BHEL Haridwar Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The application process has two steps: Candidates must first register on the National Apprenticeship Training Portal at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in . All details, including the Aadhaar number, must be entered carefully. The name, father's name, and date of birth in Aadhaar must match exactly, and the portal must show successful Aadhaar verification. After registering on the Apprenticeship Portal, candidates must complete the online application form under the link "Engagement of Trade Apprenticeship for 2026, BHEL Haridwar" on the BHEL Haridwar career website: hwr.bhel.com/recruitment . Fill in all personal details accurately, including name, father's name, date of birth, category, High School and ITI marks. Upload a recent passport-size photograph (.jpg/.jpeg, up to 30 KB). Upload the required documents in PDF format (up to 500 KB). The last date to submit the online application is 15 July 2026 . After successfully submitting the online form, candidates must take a printout of the Acknowledgement Slip and send it, along with self-attested photocopies of the required documents, in an envelope marked "Trade Apprentice Training 2026 Application Form and Trade Name" to the address below by post or in person, on or before 27 July 2026 : Karyapalak (Ma.Sam.) Room No. 29, Human Resources – Recruitment Section Main Administrative Building, BHEL, HEEP Ranipur, Haridwar (Uttarakhand) – 249403

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions for Applicants
content_sections.text: Applications received without complete details, without the required uploaded documents, or without submission of the acknowledgement slip and documents will be rejected. Candidates who have already completed apprenticeship training and were terminated from another organization within three months prior to joining BHEL are not eligible under the Apprentices (Amendment) Rules, 2025. Dependents (spouse/children) of BHEL Haridwar employees get a 3-year relaxation in the ITI passing year requirement, subject to RDSDE online approval; registration on the Apprenticeship Portal is still mandatory. No travelling allowance or dearness allowance will be paid to candidates called for joining/reporting. Selected candidates must make their own arrangements for stay and food during the training period. This training does not confer any right or claim to regular employment at BHEL. Any corrigendum, correction, or update related to this recruitment will be published only on the official BHEL Haridwar career website; no separate notice will be given through newspapers or other media. Management reserves the right to modify, postpone, cancel, or suspend the selection process at any time without assigning any reason, and its decision will be final. Any dispute arising out of this notification will fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of courts/tribunals in Haridwar.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in BHEL Haridwar Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 187 vacancies across seven trades: Fitter, Machinist, Turner, Welder, Electrician, Draughtsman (Mechanical), and Foundryman.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply online for BHEL Haridwar Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 15 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for BHEL Haridwar Trade Apprentice?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed High School and ITI in the relevant trade from an NCVT (or SCVT for Welder and Electrician trades) recognized institute, in the year 2023, 2024, 2025, or 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for BHEL Haridwar Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years. The maximum age is 27 years for General category, 30 years for OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), and 32 years for SC/ST candidates, as on 01 July 2026. PwD candidates get an additional 10 years of relaxation.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee for BHEL Haridwar Trade Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official notification does not mention any application fee for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q6. How is the merit list prepared for BHEL Haridwar Apprentice selection?
faq.answer: The merit list is prepared using a weighted average: 70% weightage to ITI marks and 30% weightage to High School marks.

faq.question: Q7. Where do I submit the documents after applying online?
faq.answer: Selected applicants must send the acknowledgement slip along with self-attested documents by post or in person to the Human Resources – Recruitment Section, BHEL, HEEP, Ranipur, Haridwar, Uttarakhand – 249403, by 27 July 2026.

faq.question: Q8. Does BHEL Haridwar apprenticeship guarantee a permanent job?
faq.answer: No. As per the official notification, completion of this apprenticeship training does not confer any right or claim to regular employment at BHEL.
